entities.name: BARLEY
entities.description: Two-rowed genotypes studied for genotype-by-environment interaction in the Mediterranean region

entities.name: MEDITERRANEAN REGION
entities.description: The geographical area where the barley genotypes were grown and studied

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The primary agricultural productivity measure, specifically grain yield per environment

entities.name: GENOTYPE-BY-ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between genetic makeup and environmental factors affecting the performance of barley

entities.name: SYRIA
entities.description: One of the countries in the Mediterranean region where the study was conducted

entities.name: NORTH AFRICA
entities.description: One of the regions in the Mediterranean where the study was conducted

entities.name: GROWTH TYPE
entities.description: The classification of barley genotypes as either winter or spring types

entities.name: HEADING DATE
entities.description: The timing of the heading stage in barley genotypes, categorized by earliness

entities.name: HIGH-YIELDING ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments characterized by higher average grain yields

entities.name: LOW-YIELDING ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments characterized by lower average grain yields

entities.name: CLUSTER ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to classify genotypes and environments into groups

entities.name: ADDITIVE MAIN EFFECTS AND MULTIPLICATIVE INTERACTION MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model used to analyze the genotype-by-environment interaction

entities.name: SEASONAL RAINFALL
entities.description: An environmental factor that influences the classification of environments into high or low yielding

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: An environmental factor that influences the classification of environments into high or low yielding

entities.name: HARSH ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments with large variation between seasons and locations that hamper yield selection

relationships.source: ADDITIVE MAIN EFFECTS AND MULTIPLICATIVE INTERACTION MODEL
